What is Particle Board?

Engineered Wood for Every Need

Particle board (chipboard) is an engineered wood product manufactured from wood chips, sawdust, shavings, and agricultural residues like bagasse, rice husk, and cotton stalk — bonded using synthetic resin adhesives under heat and pressure.

It is one of the most cost-effective and versatile panel products used in furniture manufacturing, interior fittings, flooring underlay, and construction.

India produces both plain and pre-laminated particle boards with surface finishes including melamine, veneer, marble-finish laminates, and PVC foil.

✔ Flat Pressed

Standard boards pressed in flat multi-daylight presses for uniform density

✔ Extruded

Produced by extrusion, lighter weight, ideal for core materials

✔ MR Grade

Moisture-resistant variety for kitchens and humid environments

✔ Pre-Laminated

Ready-to-use boards with decorative melamine or PVC surface finish

Gabon, Africa

Okoume Face Veneer — Straight from the Source

OkoumeGreen imports premium Okoume face veneer directly from Gabon, Africa. We manage the entire supply chain — from certified sustainable forests to processing and global export.

About Okoume

Africa's Finest Decorative Hardwood

Okoume (Aucoumea klaineana) is a tropical hardwood native to Gabon and the Congo Basin. Prized for its silky pink-salmon hue, fine uniform grain, and excellent peelability, it is the preferred species for high-quality face veneers used in marine-grade plywood, furniture panels, and decorative surfaces worldwide.

As a company bearing its name, OkoumeGreen is deeply committed to responsible and traceable sourcing from certified Gabonese forests.

🌳 Aucoumea Klaineana Gabon, Africa Face Veneer FSC Traceable Marine Grade

Supply Routes
From Our Operations

Our Forestry & Veneer Operations in Gabon

From the forest roads where our log convoys travel, to the warehouses where premium Okoume veneer is stacked and prepared for global shipment.

🚛 Log Transport

Convoy of Okoume Logs — Gabon Roads

Our log transport convoys carry freshly felled Okoume timber from certified forest concessions to processing facilities and ports across Gabon.

🪵 Veneer Stock

Okoume Face Veneer — Ready for Export

Palletised bundles of premium Okoume face veneer graded, strapped and export-ready for plywood mills in India, Italy, China and France.

Our Value Chain

From Gabon's Forests to Your Factory

🌿
1

Sustainable Forestry

We source from responsibly managed Okoume forests in Gabon. Only mature trees are selected following FSC-aligned guidelines, ensuring long-term regeneration of the ecosystem.

🪓
2

Log Selection & Felling

Trained foresters identify and fell premium Okoume logs meeting strict diameter and quality criteria. Each log is traceable to its source concession.

🚛
3

Transport of Logs

Our convoys of heavy trucks transport Okoume logs from forest concessions through Gabon's roads to port facilities at Port-Gentil or Libreville for export.

🏭
4

Veneer Transformation

Logs are rotary-peeled into thin face veneer sheets at Gabonese facilities, then carefully dried, graded and palletised — ready for plywood and panel manufacturing.

📋
5

Quality Grading & Export

Each bundle is graded by thickness, grain consistency, and colour uniformity. Phytosanitary certificates and CITES compliance documents are issued before containerised shipment.

🤝
6

Delivery to Customers

Premium Okoume face veneer is delivered to our customers in India, Italy, China, and France — serving plywood mills, furniture makers and joinery manufacturers.

🌸

Pink-Salmon Hue

Distinctive warm colour prized in decorative panels and furniture faces

🪵

Fine Uniform Grain

Consistent grain ideal for rotary peeling and face veneer production

⚖️

Lightweight

Low density makes finished panels easy to handle and work with

🛥️

Marine Grade

Approved for BS 1088 marine plywood — highest durability standard

♻️

Sustainably Sourced

Traceable from certified Gabonese concessions with responsible forestry

How It's Made

Particle Board Production Process

A highly controlled, multi-step manufacturing process ensures consistent quality, structural integrity and compliance with IS standards.

1

Raw Material

Wood chips, sawdust, agro-residues sourced and collected at the plant.

2

Drying

Rotary drum dryers reduce moisture to 2–4% for optimal bonding.

3

Particle Sizing

Chippers and screens separate fine surface and coarse core fractions.

4

Resin Blending

UF or MUF resin with hardener blended with particles in glue blenders.

5

Mat Forming

Resin-coated particles spread in face, core, back layers via air-jet formers.

6

Pre-Pressing

Low-pressure pre-press reduces mat thickness and improves handling.

7

Hot Pressing

Hydraulic presses at 160–220°C and 20–30 kg/cm² cure and form panels.

8

Cooling

Star coolers and conditioning stabilise internal stresses in boards.

9

Cutting & Sanding

Trimmed to size and sanded for precise thickness and smooth finish.

10

QC & Dispatch

MOR, MOE, IB, swelling & formaldehyde tests per IS 3087 before dispatch.
